name: Mulhacén Ascent Trail
name_original: Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada – ruta sur circular al Pico Mulhacén por las Siete Lagunas
description: Hike the difficult 10.9-mile (17.6 km) circular route to Pico Mulhacén via the Siete Lagunas in Sierra Nevada National Park.
region_intro: Hiking around Pórtugos offers access to the diverse landscapes of the Sierra Nevada Natural Park, situated at 1303 meters above sea level. The region is characterized by mountain scenery, pine forests, ravines, and terraced slopes. Hikers can explore a well-established network of trails that traverse varied terrain, including the iron-rich Fuente de Agria and traditional Alpujarran villages. Best hiking trails around Pórtugos The most popular hiking route is Hoya del Portillo and Loma del Cascajar Negro Loop, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete, offering panoramic mountain vistas within the Sierra Nevada. Another top favourite among local hikers is Medieval Path of La Alpujarra – circular between Busquístar, Pórtugos, Atalbéitar and Ferreirola (PR-A 299), a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This trail connects neighboring villages and winds through agricultural fields with views of the River Trevélez gorge. Local hikers also love the Mulhacén Ascent Trail, a 10.9 miles (17.6 km) trail leading to the highest peak in the Iberian Peninsula, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around Pórtugos is defined by mountain scenery, pine forests, and traditional village architecture. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging high-altitude treks to gentle village strolls. The routes in Pórtugos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1600 reviews. More than 7000 hikers have used komoot to explore Pórtugos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Pórtugos?
A: There are nearly 200 hiking trails around Pórtugos, offering a wide range of options from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents within the Sierra Nevada Natural Park.

Q: Are there any notable natural features or waterfalls to see on hikes near Pórtugos?
A: Yes, a significant natural attraction is the &lt;strong&gt;Fuente de Agria&lt;/strong&gt;, an iron-rich mineral spring that creates a beautiful orange waterfall and colors the riverbed due to its high iron concentration. It's located near the Ermita de la Virgen de las Angustias chapel. Another spot is the &lt;strong&gt;Merendero de la Junta de los Ríos&lt;/strong&gt;, a picnic area next to a waterfall in the 'Tajo Cortes', surrounded by pine forests.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Pórtugos?
A: The hiking routes in Pórtugos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1600 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from breathtaking mountain scenery and pine forests to the unique terraced slopes and traditional Alpujarran villages.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Pórtugos?
A: Yes, Pórtugos offers several easier trails suitable for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980683108/la-taha-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tahá Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a shorter, moderate option at 5.01 km, perfect for a relaxed hike through ancient whitewashed villages and scenic landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in Pórtugos?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980639286/hoya-del-portillo-and-loma-del-cascajar-negro-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoya del Portillo and Loma del Cascajar Negro Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.5 km trail offering panoramic mountain vistas. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980497755/medieval-path-of-la-alpujarra-circular-between-busquistar-portugos-atalbeitar-and-ferreirola-pr-a-299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Path of La Alpujarra (PR-A 299)&lt;/a&gt;, a 7.1 km route connecting Pórtugos with neighboring villages.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Pórtugos?
A: The terrain around Pórtugos is varied, characterized by the rugged mountain nature of the Alpujarra region. You'll encounter mountain scenery, pine forests, ravines, and terraced slopes. Trails range from well-maintained paths to more challenging, rocky sections, especially on higher-altitude routes.

Q: Can I hike to Mulhacén, the highest peak, from Pórtugos?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984019771/mulhacen-ascent-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mulhacén Ascent Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17.6 km circular route that leads to Mulhacén, the highest peak in the Iberian Peninsula (3479 meters), offering impressive views of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are there any trails that pass through traditional villages or offer cultural insights?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980497755/medieval-path-of-la-alpujarra-circular-between-busquistar-portugos-atalbeitar-and-ferreirola-pr-a-299" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval Path of La Alpujarra (PR-A 299)&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, winding through agricultural fields and connecting Pórtugos with neighboring villages like Busquístar, Atalbéitar, and Ferreirola. These villages feature traditional Berber-style architecture with flat-roofed houses and narrow, twisting streets.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or points of interest near the hiking trails?
A: Beyond the natural features, you can find several interesting points. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/111802" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mulhacén Summit&lt;/a&gt; offers incredible views. For those interested in high-altitude shelters, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670167" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carihuela Bivouac Shelter&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/670151" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vivac de la Caldera Shelter&lt;/a&gt; are notable. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/181587" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hondera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Pórtugos?
A: Pórtugos, situated at 1303 meters above sea level, offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are generally ideal, with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be warm, especially at lower altitudes, while winter offers opportunities for snow hiking in higher parts of the Sierra Nevada.

Q: Are there any trails that feature acequias (irrigation channels)?
A: Yes, the region is known for its historical irrigation channels. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980711586/pitres-acequia-gorda-portugos-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pitres - Acequia Gorda - Pórtugos Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.9 km route that specifically passes by acequias and waterfalls, showcasing this traditional aspect of the Alpujarran landscape.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads in Pórtugos using public transport?
A: While specific public transport routes directly to all trailheads might be limited, Pórtugos itself is accessible by bus, connecting it to larger towns in the Alpujarra region. From the village, many trails are within walking distance or a short local transfer. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.
tips: Alto del Chorrillo is the starting point for those who crown the Mulhacén in One-Day Routes. There is a shuttle bus organized by the Altas Cumbres Interpretation Service that goes from Capileira to this point. From here to the summit there are less than 6 kilometers.
Ascent or descent south side to Mulhacén with magnificent panorama to the Mediterranean.
Pre-summit of the Mulhacén peak with a magnificent panoramic view to the south. On clear days you can see Africa.

The area of the Seven Lagoons (and Laguna Hondera in particular) is one of the most beautiful places in the Sierra Nevada National Park. The climb up here next to the waterfall formed by the Chorreras Negras is demanding, but all the pain in your legs is forgotten as soon as you reach the top and see the beauty of the place.

Important note: if you plan to spend the night here, do not pitch your tent in the borreguiles (the grasslands). They are home to several species of flora endemic to the Sierra Nevada and it is necessary to protect them.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the southern circular route to Pico Mulhacén via the Siete Lagunas?
faqs.answer: The full circular route is approximately 17.6 km (10.9 miles) and is estimated to take around 7 hours and 16 minutes of moving time. However, the ascent via Cañada de las Siete Lagunas from Trevélez is often cited as taking around 13 hours for experienced hikers due to the significant elevation gain and challenging terrain. Plan for a full day or consider an overnight stay.

faqs.question: What are the parking options and where does the trail typically start?
faqs.answer: The route commonly starts from Trevélez, which is known as the highest village in Spain's municipal area. You'll find parking available in or near Trevélez. From there, the path leads out of the village and into the mountains.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the way to Pico Mulhacén via Siete Lagunas?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and demanding. Initially, from Trevélez, you might encounter cobbled paths through farms. As you ascend, it quickly becomes a steep, rough, and unrelenting trail. You'll navigate through alpine meadows, cross streams, and encounter rocky sections, especially when climbing towards Laguna Hondera and the higher elevations. It transitions from lush valleys to stark, high-mountain environments.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along this route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the stunning Alto del Chorrillo, the iconic Southern Route to Mulhacén, and of course, the Mulhacén Summit itself, offering panoramic views that can extend to the Mediterranean Sea on clear days. The Siete Lagunas glacial cirque, with its seven high-altitude lakes, particularly Hondera Lagoon, is a major draw, known for its 'Alpine feel' and vibrant green meadows.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or less experienced hikers?
faqs.answer: No, this route is consistently described as difficult and demanding. It requires a very good level of fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy hiking boots, and often alpine experience. The significant elevation gain and long duration make it unsuitable for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route to Pico Mulhacén?
faqs.answer: The optimal months for this hike are typically May to November. However, conditions can change rapidly, and earlier in the season (May-June), snow and ice may still be present, requiring appropriate gear like crampons and an ice axe. Always check current weather and trail conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: While there isn't a general entrance fee for Sierra Nevada National Park, certain activities or access to specific areas might require permits, especially during peak seasons or for guided tours. For this particular route, it's always best to check the official Sierra Nevada National Park website for the latest regulations regarding hiking, wild camping (which is popular in the Siete Lagunas area), and any potential access restrictions.

faqs.question: Is the Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada – southern circular route to Pico Mulhacén via the Siete Lagunas dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Sierra Nevada is a National Park, and regulations regarding dogs can vary. Generally, dogs are allowed on trails but must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other hikers. It's advisable to check the specific rules for the Parque Nacional de Sierra Nevada before bringing your dog, especially given the challenging nature of this particular route.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife might I spot along the trail?
faqs.answer: The Sierra Nevada is home to diverse wildlife. On this route, you have a good chance of spotting ibex (mountain goats) in the rocky crags and around areas like Laguna Hondera. Keep an eye out for various bird species as well.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails in Sierra Nevada National Park?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the "Subida al Mulhacén" trail and also includes parts of the "Transnevada Ruta H" and the "Integral de Sierra Nevada" trails. You'll also find sections of the "Trevélez-Alto de Chorrillo" and "Trevélez-Siete Lagunas" trails integrated into this circular route.

faqs.question: Are there any specific safety considerations for hiking at high altitude on Mulhacén?
faqs.answer: Yes, Mulhacén is the highest peak in mainland Spain (3,479m), so altitude can be a significant factor. Be aware of symptoms of altitude sickness, stay hydrated, and ascend gradually if possible. The weather can change very quickly at these elevations, so be prepared for strong winds, cold temperatures, and sudden storms, even in summer. Always carry appropriate layers, navigation tools, and sufficient food and water.
